untranslatable_reason: Signal requires monitoring of interest rate changes and arbitrage-specific metrics (conversion/reversal arbitrage relationships, call pricing relative to interest rates, arbitrage profit calculations). Interest rates are not available in the allowed indicators list, and arbitrage strategy execution conditions cannot be expressed using price-based technical indicators alone. This signal requires external macro data (interest rate levels/changes) and options pricing model inputs that are outside the scope of a price/volume-based backtest engine.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_193 describes a regime-dependent relationship between interest rates and option arbitrage profitability. The core insight (call prices rise when rates rise in conversion/reversal arbitrage) depends on: (1) interest rate monitoring (not available), (2) options pricing relationships (not available), and (3) arbitrage strategy state (not available). The signal is context-dependent and regime-agnostic (ANY regime), but lacks the fundamental data inputs needed for systematic translation.
